jokes.text: Oh, Lord. Hiking is always such a strain on the buttocks.
jokes.setup: Character commenting on the physical toll of hiking
jokes.punchline: Focus on buttocks rather than typical complaints like feet or legs
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection - formal language contrasted with focus on buttocks creates mild humor

jokes.text: Don't tell me you actually believe in Bigfoot, you blathering ninny-Hammer.
jokes.punchline: Ninny-hammer - archaic insult combined with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that fits Bender's character - archaic term with modern attitude

jokes.text: Bigfoot's my hero. Growing up, he was the celebrity I most identified with... Because he was a loner who hated the popular monsters yet longed to be one.
jokes.punchline: Bigfoot as celebrity with monster social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - applying modern social dynamics to mythical creatures, shows his character depth

jokes.text: Enough emotions. This isn't a fat camp, for God's sake.
jokes.punchline: Random comparison to fat camp
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender dismissiveness with unexpected comparison

jokes.text: Although you wouldn't know it from looking.
jokes.punchline: Immediate cruel follow-up observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender cruelty - can't resist the follow-up insult. Great economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Bender, if you want to sleep in the tent tonight... You're welcome to join me and Hermes for a little 'just friends' spooning.
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable specificity about spooning arrangement
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor inappropriate oversharing - the quotes around 'just friends' make it worse

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Ranger Park, the park ranger. I get it.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his own ridiculous punny name
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about character naming convention - acknowledging the pun directly

jokes.text: In the dense forests of the Pacific Northwest... Dwells the strange and beautiful creature known as Bigfoot, perhaps.
jokes.punchline: Undermining certainty with 'perhaps'
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of nature documentaries - undermining authority with casual uncertainty

jokes.text: Today threaten what might possibly be his habitat. Although if it's not, they don't.
jokes.punchline: Logical but absurd qualification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of documentary uncertainty - technically true but completely useless information

jokes.text: They typically dwell just behind rocks, but are also sometimes playful... Bounding into thick fogs and out-of-focus areas.
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to why Bigfoot photos are always blurry
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke explaining why all Bigfoot evidence is poor quality - treats it as natural behavior

jokes.text: It should say 'Top Quality Exercycle For Sale.' And could you put 'Top Quality' in bold? You can't? Okay, whatever.
jokes.punchline: Ranger making personal classified ad during nature film
jokes.explanation: Absurd interruption of serious documentary for mundane personal business

jokes.text: Bigfoot is a crucial part of the ecosystem, if he exists. So let's all help keep Bigfoot possibly alive... For future generations to enjoy, unless he doesn't exist.
jokes.punchline: Constant hedging undermining the entire point
jokes.explanation: Perfect conclusion to the uncertainty theme - conservation message completely undermined by doubt

jokes.text: But I do see him each night in my dreams... And each day in the smiling faces of hairy children.
jokes.setup: Ranger asked if he's seen Bigfoot
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable overly-poetic response about hairy children
jokes.explanation: Creepy ranger character establishing moment - inappropriate romanticism about hairy children

jokes.text: I have some of someone who saw him.
jokes.setup: Bender demands Bigfoot droppings as evidence
jokes.punchline: Ranger has droppings from someone who saw Bigfoot, not Bigfoot himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic error - misunderstanding what would constitute evidence

jokes.text: Oh, he's gone. He said you should keep wasting your life, though.
jokes.punchline: Cruel fake message from imaginary Bigfoot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender cruelty - using fake Bigfoot sighting to deliver insult

jokes.text: What you saw was Bigfoot the monster truck. But thanks for a great question.
jokes.setup: Claim about seeing Bigfoot crushing cars
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation with fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke with patronizing follow-up

jokes.text: That would be very expensive... And most people who believe in Bigfoot are broke.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to set up video cameras
jokes.punchline: Insulting believers while explaining budget constraints
jokes.explanation: Casual insult disguised as practical explanation

jokes.text: Where? Up your face. Everybody do the Bender.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical insult plus dance move
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender randomness - weak insult structure but fits character

jokes.text: That guy won't be going home to his kids.
jokes.punchline: Darkly personalizing insect death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender dark humor - anthropomorphizing insects for casual cruelty

jokes.text: Fry. Fry, wake up. It's me, Bigface. Come out and groom my mangy fur.
jokes.punchline: Obvious alien pretending to be 'Bigface' with poor disguise
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection - obviously fake Bigfoot voice with weird specific request

jokes.text: Bigfoot? You taught yourself English?
jokes.setup: Encountering what he thinks is Bigfoot
jokes.punchline: Fry's first concern is language acquisition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - missing the obvious while focusing on minor details

jokes.text: I bet you have a wounded raccoon friend... That you tenderly nurse back to health while you go: [makes sounds] In the end, they shoot you, but you teach us about things.
jokes.punchline: Complete movie monster archetype breakdown
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry movie reference knowledge applied to real situation - knows all the tropes

jokes.text: Why does your vanity plate say 'Probe 1'?
jokes.punchline: Noticing vanity plate detail during abduction
jokes.explanation: Great observation during crisis - aliens having vanity plates is absurd, noticing during abduction is very Fry

jokes.text: Bender, wasn't that Fry's tent? 'Bender.'
jokes.punchline: Bender's nonchalant one-word response to friend's disappearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender callousness - saying his own name instead of showing concern. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Fry, what in Sega Genesis happened to you?
jokes.punchline: Using video game console as an exclamation
jokes.explanation: Creative future-appropriate exclamation - using old gaming console as oath

jokes.text: Fry, you have no nose. Your nose is gone. You have no nose on your face. Where it is, I can't say... But on your face it's not.
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily repetitive and unhelpful explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg terrible bedside manner - completely unhelpful repetition masquerading as medical care

jokes.text: You chopped off your nose so you could look more like your hero: me, Bender.
jokes.punchline: Bender making it about himself despite having no nose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism - assuming everything is about him, even when it makes no sense

jokes.text: I don't get that gesture. Am I wrong?
jokes.setup: Someone makes thumbs up gesture about alien description
jokes.punchline: Fry can't see thumbs up because he has no nose to judge perspective
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to missing nose affecting his perception - solid sight gag

jokes.text: I don't want to teach a new one how to shoot milk when I laugh.
jokes.setup: Fry wants his original nose back
jokes.punchline: Specific weird personal detail about milk-shooting ability
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and gross personal detail that feels very real - perfect Fry overshare

jokes.text: My nose is an aphrodisiac? I'm gonna drop a barf.
jokes.setup: Learning nose is used as aphrodisiac
jokes.punchline: Childish expression for vomiting
jokes.explanation: Fry's childish way of expressing disgust - 'drop a barf' is appropriately immature

jokes.text: Snarfs it and does the worm.
jokes.setup: Worried about alien using his nose
jokes.punchline: Combining eating slang with dance move reference
jokes.explanation: Fry's weird combination of slang terms for eating and dancing

jokes.text: We've been to every scuzzy bazaar... In the galaxy, including Pottery Barn.
jokes.punchline: Including upscale home goods store in list of scuzzy bazaars
jokes.explanation: Great unexpected inclusion - treating Pottery Barn as disreputable marketplace

jokes.text: Welcome, friends. How may I pervert you?
jokes.punchline: Pervert instead of serve - appropriate for adult business
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that establishes character and setting efficiently

jokes.text: Of course not. In fact, he's a crook. Yep. Stolen Pez, anyone?
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately offering to sell stolen candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - can't resist criminal opportunity even when trying to blend in

jokes.text: Human horn. So fresh you can still see the eyeglass marks.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing evidence of recent human ownership
jokes.explanation: Dark humor detail that makes the horror more real - specific evidence of victim

jokes.text: I videotape everyone who comes in here so I can blackmail them later.
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting criminal intent
jokes.explanation: Casual admission of evil plan - straightforward villain honesty

jokes.text: Hey, I'm a porno-dealing monster. What do I care what you think?
jokes.setup: Reacting to disgust at his behavior
jokes.punchline: Self-aware embrace of villainy
jokes.explanation: Character owns his awfulness - no pretense about moral standards

jokes.text: That's exactly the lie we used to get past your guards.
jokes.setup: Lrrr commenting on their fundraising story
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admitting it was a lie
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender honesty about dishonesty - no pretense when caught

jokes.text: Why would a virile male like Lrrr need human horn? I don't even know what it's for. What is it... Something you put in salad dressing?
jokes.punchline: Obvious overcompensation and clueless suggestions
jokes.explanation: Transparent denial with increasingly ridiculous alternative uses

jokes.text: Like you've ever seen a salad. My weight is appropriate and attractive.
jokes.punchline: Wife's cruel comeback followed by defensive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect married couple dynamic - cruel accurate observation plus defensive denial

jokes.text: I guess I'll just go home and marry a skunk.
jokes.setup: Giving up on getting his nose back
jokes.punchline: Absurd romantic alternative for noseless person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - if he can't smell, might as well embrace smelly things

jokes.text: For the last time, I don't like lilacs. Your first wife was the one who liked lilacs. She also liked to shut up.
jokes.punchline: Cruel comparison to dead wife with ominous implication
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about first wife's fate delivered as petty argument - very Futurama

jokes.text: But I thought the horn was the human wing-dang-doodle.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous euphemism for penis
jokes.explanation: Absurd alien euphemism that became quotable - perfectly silly

jokes.text: The main event, so to speak, is downstairs near the wallet.
jokes.punchline: Anatomical directions using wallet as landmark
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism using practical landmark - wallet location makes sense

jokes.text: Ever seen soccer players line up to block a free kick? They ain't covering their noses, I'll tell you that much.
jokes.setup: Explaining what body part needs protection
jokes.punchline: Soccer defensive wall covering groins not faces
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor using real sports reference to make anatomical point

jokes.text: Interesting. The trousers conceal a tiny secondary horn.
jokes.punchline: Clinical alien description of penis as secondary horn
jokes.explanation: Alien perspective on human anatomy - clinical term for intimate body part

jokes.text: Then bring it to our royal bedchamber and put it in the sock drawer... With the other things that have failed to arouse my passion for this woman.
jokes.punchline: Revealing drawer full of failed romantic aids
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about their failing relationship disguised as casual instruction

jokes.text: I'm usually the first guy to toot my own horn-- I'll say! Whoo! But in this case I don't think it's gonna do any good. That's what she said! Whoo!
jokes.punchline: Bender's double entendre reactions
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre setup - Fry's innocence creates setup for Bender's crude humor

jokes.text: Yes, but I'm the one who injured them. Oh, shush. You stepped on them by accident and then you cried all night.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lrrr helping injured animals
jokes.punchline: Revealing he caused injuries then was emotional about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character development - tough exterior hiding emotional core, with wife's protective interpretation

jokes.text: And you used to wear a size 3 cape, but not anymore!
jokes.setup: Argument about how Lrrr has changed
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about cape size indicating weight gain
jokes.explanation: Specific detail about relationship decline - cape size as metaphor for letting himself go

jokes.text: I keep my horn as long as you two end up doing the horizontal monster mash.
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism for alien sex
jokes.explanation: Creative dance-based euphemism appropriate for alien characters

jokes.text: Why don't you just inject some fat... Straight into your ass and cut out the middleman?
jokes.punchline: Cruel suggestion about direct fat injection
jokes.explanation: Brutal efficiency suggestion that highlights marital cruelty

jokes.text: Bang. Zoom. Straight to the third moon of Omicron Persei 8.
jokes.punchline: Alien version of 'to the moon' threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect alien adaptation of classic TV threat - maintains domestic abuse comedy while making it sci-fi

jokes.text: It's used to it! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Lrrr threatening to have Fry's horn 'jerked'
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude interpretation and celebration
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crude humor - finding sexual interpretation in threatening situation

jokes.text: Boy, who knew a cooler could also make a handy wang coffin.
jokes.setup: Preparing container for severed body part
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about dual-purpose container
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about severed genitals delivered with casual product review tone

jokes.text: Yes! I never thought I'd escape with my doodle, but I pulled it out. Just like at the movie theater! Whoo!
jokes.punchline: Innocent celebration with unintended sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry innocent double entendre - doesn't realize what he's saying

jokes.text: So I can chop off his feet as proof he exists... Then dump him back in the wild. He'll do fine.
jokes.punchline: Casual plan for mutilation with optimistic follow-up
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mutilation delivered with complete nonchalance - 'he'll do fine' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: This human's lower horn is one of God's creatures. A living thing. And all living things, large and small-- In this case, small! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Lrrr's noble speech about respecting life
jokes.punchline: Bender interrupting to comment on size
jokes.explanation: Bender interrupting moral speech with crude size joke - perfect character moment

jokes.text: As usual! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Fry getting to keep his lower horn
jokes.punchline: Bender's continuing commentary on Fry's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Running gag conclusion - efficient but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Well, Fry, it looks like you get to hold on to your lower horn. As usual! Whoo!
jokes.punchline: Bender's masturbation reference
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual reference but perfectly timed for Bender. Character consistency over originality.

jokes.text: You ask a passerby for directions... Only to find he has no face or something.
jokes.punchline: Vague, uncertain horror description
jokes.explanation: Parody of Twilight Zone with intentionally vague horror - 'or something' undermines the fear

jokes.text: I have combined the DNA of the world's most evil animals... To make the most evil creature of them all. It turns out it's man.
jokes.setup: Mad scientist creating ultimate evil creature
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a regular human
jokes.explanation: Classic dark humor misdirection - building up to reveal humans are naturally the most evil
